title: OpenZeppelin Contracts for Sui — AI Integrator Module Metadata, v1.0
description: Schema for per-module YAML metadata files under `<package-path>/llms/<...>/<module>.yaml`. Designed to let AI integrator agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline, Copilot, Aider, Gemini CLI, etc.) ground code generation at the schema level rather than re-deriving structure from Move source on every call. See llms.txt at the repository root for the discovery convention.

schema_version.description: Schema version. v1.0 is the first public release of this format.

name.description: Fully-qualified Move module declaration name in the form `openzeppelin_<package>::<module>`.

package.description: Package name as it appears in `Move.toml`'s `[package].name` field.

one_liner.description: Single sentence (≤240 chars) capturing WHAT the module does and any load-bearing constraint. AI agents read this first to decide if the module fits the task at hand. Long-form prose belongs in `summary`.

summary.description: Optional multi-paragraph elaboration of the module — context that doesn't fit in `one_liner`. AI agents read this when the one_liner is not enough to decide whether and how to use the module.

re_exports_from.description: Module names (within the same package, no package prefix) whose APIs this module re-exports via `public use fun`. Optional — emit only when re-exports exist; agents reading the module surface should also consult these YAMLs to see the full reachable API.

domain.description: Top-level functional category of the module (kebab-case). Current categories in the library include `access-control`, `math`, and others as packages ship. The pattern is open so new categories don't require a schema bump.

frequency.description: Call-frequency model. `admin-frequency` = invoked from governance / setup paths; shared-object writes acceptable. `user-frequency` = invoked from per-transaction hot paths; shared-object writes serialize the protocol and must be avoided. `mixed` = both, with case-by-case guidance in `decisions[]`.

does_not_solve.description: One-line bullets stating what the module deliberately does NOT cover. Points integrators at sibling modules or alternative libraries.

mvr.description: Move Package Registry slug for this module's containing package.

repo_ref.description: Full 40-character commit SHA in OpenZeppelin/contracts-sui that the YAML was extracted from. Never a branch name or tag — branch/tag refs are resolved to a SHA by the staging script.

release.description: Release tag this YAML matches (semver-prefixed, e.g., `"v1.2.0"` or `"v1.2.0-rc.1"`) when extracted at a tagged release. `null` when extracted from main between tags. In either case, `install.repo_ref` is the actual reproducible pin.

use_statement.description: Move `use` statement(s) the integrator copies into their consumer module.

move_toml_snippet.description: `Move.toml` `[dependencies]` snippet pinning the module's package via MVR.

github_alternative.description: Alternative `Move.toml` `[dependencies]` snippet pinning the package via direct git ref. The `<commit-sha-or-tag>` placeholder is the agent-fills-in slot — `install.repo_ref` documents which commit the YAML describes.

setup_snippet.description: Move code block showing the minimal scaffold an integrator copies to start using the module (e.g., the consumer module declaration plus first integration calls).

quick_start.description: Minimal end-to-end happy-path Move code — 3-5 calls demonstrating the typical sequence after init.

types.description: Integrator-facing types the module introduces. Empty array means the module is stateless / introduces no new types.

errors.description: Map of error constant names (e.g., `EUnauthorized`) to their abort code and message. Cross-module errors that surface to the caller through macro expansion (e.g., from `openzeppelin_math::macros`) are documented inline here.

decisions.description: Decision points an integrator faces when using the module. Each entry is a question with at least two options — gives the AI agent a structured branch instead of inferring from prose.

must.description: Natural-language requirement the caller must satisfy. Written as an imperative statement (e.g., "Pass a non-zero `denominator` to `mul_div`.").

fails_with.description: Error constant name (e.g., `EDivideByZero`) that aborts when the precondition is violated. Must match a key in `errors:`.

affects.description: Mechanical union of every `api[].name` whose `aborts` list references this error code.

do_not.description: Anti-patterns. Each entry documents an integration mistake the AI agent should not generate.

composes_with.description: Modules this one composes with in real protocols. Empty array is a valid value; populate as composition patterns are validated. Cross-module composability queries are the natural fit for an MCP server — see the project's MCP roadmap.

file.description: Repo-relative path to the test file containing the canonical happy-path test.

function.description: Test function name to load as the canonical end-to-end example.

canonical_test.description: Pointer to a single end-to-end happy-path test for the module — the test an agent should read to ground its mental model of the module. `null` when no single test serves this role (e.g., per-function sharded suites).

precondition_fails_with.description: Error constant name (matching one of the `fails_with` values in `preconditions[]`).

test_file.description: Repo-relative path to the test file containing the proof tests.

test_functions.description: Test function names that prove the precondition aborts when violated. Empty array for consumer-side preconditions with no in-tree proof.

precondition_proofs.description: Test-grounded proofs that each precondition actually triggers its abort code.

_audit_grounding.description: Audit-grounding section — optional, opt-in for agents that want test-pinned proof of preconditions and anti-patterns. Generation-focused agents can skip the entire block.
